title: Splitting of Iwasawa Modules and Leopoldt Conjecture
speaker: Jean-Pierre Wintenberger
abstract: Let p be an odd prime number and let F be a totally real field. Let F_cyc be the cyclotomic extension of F generated by the roots of unity of order a power of p . From the maximal abelian extension of F_cyc which is unramified (resp. unramified outside auxiliary primes), we get exact sequences of Iwasawa modules. We will discuss how splitting of these exact sequences are linked to Leopoldt conjecture for F and p . (JW with C. Khare)

proof_stages.label: Leopoldt via local units and p-adic L-values
proof_stages.summary: After fixing a totally real field F, an odd prime p, and the cyclotomic Z_p-extension F_infinity/F, the speaker recalls equivalent forms of Leopoldt: uniqueness of the Z_p-extension, maximal rank of the closure of global units in local principal units at p, nonvanishing of the p-adic regulator, and the expected pole behavior of the p-adic zeta function.

proof_stages.label: Iwasawa modules and the unramified Kummer obstruction
proof_stages.summary: The maximal abelian pro-p extensions of F_infinity unramified outside p and unramified everywhere define Lambda-modules Y_infinity and X_infinity, and Iwasawa duality identifies the relevant part with an odd class-group module, giving the criterion that Leopoldt is equivalent to the nonexistence of unramified Kummer Z_p-extensions of F_infinity.

proof_stages.label: Auxiliary-prime exact sequence and splitting criterion
proof_stages.summary: For a finite set Q of primes away from p that are inert in F_infinity, class field theory gives an exact sequence relating the module ramified only at Q to the unramified module, with a crucial |Q|-1 contribution from units, and Leopoldt is reformulated as splitting of this sequence over Q_p or equivalently as the existence, for every suitable pair q1,q2, of a Kummer Z_p-extension ramified at the primes above q1 and q2 and unramified outside them.

proof_stages.label: Hecke-algebra framework for producing the required extensions
proof_stages.summary: The proposed construction of the ramified Kummer extensions uses Hida lambda-adic Hilbert modular forms of level Gamma_0(q1q2), the p-adic L-function order m, an Eisenstein ideal in the full Hecke algebra, and the decomposition after inverting Lambda into old and new quotients, with the intended correspondence between Hecke-algebra splitting and the class-field exact-sequence splitting.

proof_stages.label: Old and full quotients as calibration of Wiles's construction
proof_stages.summary: Applying the Ribet–Mazur–Wiles construction to the old quotient yields only the unramified Kummer extension forced by the one-prime obstruction, while applying it to the full level q1q2 algebra gives a rank m+1 object corresponding to the module allowing ramification at Q.

proof_stages.label: New quotient and final ramification condition
proof_stages.summary: For the new quotient, the relevant Eisenstein ideal is shown to be proper and, after specialization at weight zero, maximal with quotient isomorphic to Q_p; hence Wiles's representation produces semisimple Lambda-modules, and if these are ramified at q1 or q2 then the preceding splitting criterion proves Leopoldt.

examples.name: Known abelian cases of Leopoldt
examples.note: Abelian extensions of Q and abelian extensions of an imaginary quadratic field are cited as already-known instances of Leopoldt, illustrating the classical p-adic logarithm/Galois-structure methods and motivating the search for a modular criterion.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker only cites the known abelian cases of Leopoldt over Q and over imaginary quadratic fields as established by Brumer, without computing or applying any construction to them.

passes.reason: Abelian extensions form systematic infinite families used as known positive cases of Leopoldt, not as failures or classification exceptions.
Abelian extensions are a systematic known positive family for Leopoldt, not used to refute a claim and not sporadic, extremal, or degenerate.
Abelian number-field cases of Leopoldt form systematic infinite families used as known positive cases, not as failures of a claim.
Abelian extensions of Q (and of imaginary quadratic fields) form systematic infinite families of number fields cited as known positive cases of Leopoldt, not as failures of a claim.
Abelian extensions of Q or imaginary quadratic fields form systematic infinite families cited as known positive cases of Leopoldt, not as failures or sporadic/extreme/degenerate objects.
Abelian Galois extensions form a systematic highly symmetric family used as known positive cases of Leopoldt, not as a refutation.
Abelian number-field cases are cited as known positive instances of Leopoldt, forming a systematic Galois-symmetric family rather than a counterexample or sporadic/extremal/degenerate object.
Abelian extensions form a systematic highly symmetric Galois family where Leopoldt is known, cited as positive known cases rather than as failures.
used as positive known systematic cases of Leopoldt, namely abelian/Galois fields with unusually regular symmetry but not sporadic, limiting, or optimal

Abelian extensions are cited as systematic known positive cases of Leopoldt, not as obstructions; their abelian Galois structure gives a high degree of symmetry but they are not sporadic, extremal, or degenerate.
Abelian number fields are cited as positive known cases of Leopoldt, forming a systematic Galois/commutative family rather than an obstruction or sporadic/extreme case.
The abelian extensions cited are known positive cases of Leopoldt, forming systematic Galois/abelian families rather than sporadic, extremal, or degenerate examples.
